title: Harden scripted_pick_demo.py: --overwrite guard + --strict for success/HDF5 stamp
description: scripted_pick_demo.py is now the primary data-gen entry point (2026-04-27 decision) but has three silent failure modes: (1) line 249-250 unconditional dataset_path.unlink() with no overwrite guard; (2) lines 495-501 except Exception falls back to terminated[0] silently; (3) lines 540-567 HDF5 color-metadata stamping wrapped in except Exception that only logs a WARN. Add --overwrite (default False, abort if file exists) and --strict (default True, re-raise on success-term and HDF5 stamp failures).
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix train_only.sh pipeline error propagation (only checks PIPESTATUS[0])
description: train_only.sh:289-295 pipes lerobot_train | tee | parse_train_log and only checks rc=${PIPESTATUS[0]}. If parse_train_log.py crashes or skips lines, the epoch is marked successful and STEP_JSONL silently corrupts. Capture both rcs and fail epoch if either is nonzero.
close_reason: Closed

title: Verify vision inputs match SmolVLA expected parameters
description: Quick sanity check that our wrist (256x256) + third_person (512x512) RGB streams are within the resolution/dtype/channel/normalization range that the SmolVLA preprocessor and SmolVLM backbone expect. Concretely: confirm resize_imgs_with_padding=(512,512), check uint8 vs float32 contract through the lerobot pipeline, confirm channel order (RGB vs BGR), confirm normalization stats (per-image vs mean/std), check the expected aspect ratio handling (padding to square vs crop), and verify our actual rendered frames don't violate any of these. Read /home/ubuntu/code/lerobot/src/lerobot/policies/smolvla/{configuration_smolvla,modeling_smolvla,processor_smolvla}.py and trace what happens to observation.images.* between dataset load and model forward. Output: a 1-page note in reports/ confirming green-light or flagging discrepancies.
close_reason: Verified: pipeline contracts (RGB, [0,1] float32, channel-first 3xHxW, square aspect no asymmetric pad, 512x512 internal target with native third-person and 2x bilinear upsample on wrist) all hold. Token budget for task prompt OK. Report at reports/2026-04-27_smolvla_vision_input_verification.md. Greenlit unblocking 18n; flagged 4 watchouts (load_vlm_weights, freeze_vision_encoder, IDENTITY visual norm, left+top pad asymmetry) for the audit to weigh.

title: Increase wrist + third-person camera resolution
description: Bump wrist_cam (128x128) and third_person_cam (256x256) to higher resolutions so the policy gets more pixels per cube and target. Current 128x128 wrist already loses detail at >0.3m; third-person at 256x256 with the wide spawn box leaves the cube 6-12px wide, hurting localization. Update envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py CameraCfg height/width on both cameras. Verify policy still trains/runs (check tensor shapes flowing through SmolVLA preprocessor). Render a fresh sample set after the change.
close_reason: wrist 128->256 and third_person 256->512 in envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py; lerobot converter feature schema, render_demo.py side-by-side, and run_vla_closed_loop gif hstack all updated to read dimensions dynamically. Verified shapes via save_camera_samples — fresh hi-res samples in reports/camera_samples/2026-04-27_hires/

title: Build fixed-seed 30-position eval harness
description: Why: every prior run quotes a different N and seed list, so cross-run SR comparison is unreliable (Phase 0.1 of recovery_plan_2026-04-24.md). The existing run_vla_closed_loop.py already has a --cube_xy flag (line 48-53) that takes a semicolon-separated list of (x,y) pairs and cycles them. So this task is: (1) write a tiny script that samples 30 (x,y) positions from the widened cube box (X in [0.40, 0.70], Y in [-0.22, 0.22]) with np.random.seed(42), (2) save to configs/eval_seed_30.json, (3) document the eval invocation. Acceptance: same checkpoint produces byte-identical per-episode JSONL across two runs; cube positions span widened box at known seed indices. Estimate ~30 min. Parent: vla_kitting-j8q (F1).
close_reason: Fixed-seed 30-position eval harness landed. configs/eval_seed_30.json (deterministic, md5sum stable across runs), generator at scripts/orchestrate/build_eval_seed_set.py, report at reports/2026-04-26_eval_harness_fixed_seed_30.md.

title: Diagnostic & evaluation foundation
description: Goal: make every claim falsifiable. Foundation that gates all retraining work. Establishes a comparable SR metric (fixed-seed 30-position eval harness) plus three vision-conditioning diagnostics (attention-difference, trajectory-overlay, P-controller saturation) and a Run B prime post-mortem. F1 must complete before any retraining is meaningful, since (a) every prior run quotes different N and seed list so cross-run SR comparison is unreliable, and (b) we have no working test for whether the policy uses vision at all (prior peak/uniform attention metric was invalidated by register-token positional bias).
close_reason: Diagnostic & evaluation foundation epic complete. Children: vla_kitting-7ky (eval harness, fixed-seed 30 cube_xy via configs/eval_seed_30.json), vla_kitting-e9y (action variance — saturated-P-controller hypothesis FALSIFIED, ee_dx/ee_dy DIVERSE at gain=2), vla_kitting-uxt (attention-difference — INVARIANT, vision NOT being used as cube-localization signal in v4), vla_kitting-vd0 (trajectory overlay — FAN with weak depth correlation, consistent with vision-not-grounding finding), vla_kitting-hzj (Run B prime SR re-eval deferred, config-diff one-pager produced). Combined deliverable: every claim is now falsifiable + we have a clear v5+ direction (auxiliary cube-localization loss or vision-tower full fine-tune to fix the vision-grounding deficit; LoRA shrink to Run B prime regime tracked separately as vla_kitting-8ux).

title: Monitor first epoch of v5 run for loss/grad-norm regressions from batch+LR jump
description: The v5 train_only.sh defaults change two knobs at once (batch 4->16 AND LR 1e-4->1e-3) plus drop vision-tower LoRA. The audit (reports/2026-04-27_smolvla_best_practices_audit.md) explicitly recommended sweeping LR AFTER fixing batch, not simultaneously, so we need to actively watch the first epoch for instability rather than waiting for the eval cadence at epoch 10. Concretely, during the first epoch (steps 0-1000) of the next run: (1) tail logs/continual/train_steps.jsonl and check loss is monotone-decreasing or at least not diverging — flag if loss > 2x its starting value at any point or if grad_norm p95 > 100. (2) confirm GPU memory fits — if OOM at batch=16, document the fallback (batch=8, then 4) and which step it crashed on. (3) sanity check that the LoRA trainable-params count matches v5 expectations: lm_expert q,k,v,o,gate,up,down + state_proj + action_in_proj + action_out_proj (full FT) + action_time_mlp_{in,out} — vision tower should be 0 trainable params. Output: short note appended to the v5 run diary OR a new dated report under reports/. If anything fires, halt the run (touch logs/continual/STOP) and decide whether to back off LR or batch first.

title: Spatial-aware vision probe at NEW camera config — gates v5 aux-loss decision
description: Why: vla_kitting-k98 probe of base SmolVLA vision features at the OLD camera config (256×256, narrow FOV, N=24) returned VERDICT=MISSING (R² < 0 on all three readouts; mean-pool features had cos-sim 0.97 across frames, basically constant). But the result is suggestive-not-conclusive: N=24 is data-starved for a 768D probe, and mean-pool washes out spatial info that may be encoded per-patch. Before committing v5 to either 'frozen vision is fine, just train the action head' OR 'vision needs aux loss', re-run the probe under the NEW camera config (512×512, repositioned at (1.5, -0.10, 0.80), FOV ~60°) with a spatial-aware aggregator and N≥100 samples. What: (1) render N=100 frames at randomized cube positions across the widened spawn box using a small wrapper around scripts/validate/render_cube_grid_for_attn_diff.py (extend to non-grid random sampling and include cube_pos in the manifest), (2) for each frame derive the empirical homography from world to image via two known anchors (magenta target at world (0.65, 0.20), cube at known forced position) so we can project the cube center into image patch coordinates accurately at the new camera config, (3) for each frame extract the patch features overlapping the cube center (e.g. 3×3 patches around the projected cube pixel) and the patch features at a fixed control region (image center) — these are the spatial-aware features, (4) run three readouts: (a) leave-one-out ridge regression from on-cube-patch features → cube_xy, R² per axis; (b) on-cube-patch vs off-cube-patch cosine similarity (should be < 0.9 if the cube is locally distinguishable); (c) cluster on-cube features by recorded cube_color, measure within-color vs across-color silhouette score (vision should at minimum separate the 5 colors). Acceptance / verdict thresholds: PASS ('vision encodes cube, frozen-vision viable') iff readout (a) R² ≥ 0.7 AND readout (b) on-cube/off-cube cos-sim < 0.9; PARTIAL iff one passes; MISSING otherwise. The verdict drives the v5 plan in vla_kitting-8ux: PASS → run v5 WITHOUT aux loss; PARTIAL → run v5 WITH aux loss at modest weight (~0.05× action loss); MISSING → run v5 WITH aux loss at higher weight (~0.1× action loss) AND consider unfreezing vision tower with a 10× lower LR. Estimate ~30 min coding + ~10 min compute. Reference: reports/2026-04-27_vision_feature_probe.md.

title: Audit our SmolVLA setup against documentation + papers for best-practice violations
description: Before the next training run, sanity-check our config against authoritative SmolVLA guidance (paper, lerobot README, lerobot smolvla examples, HuggingFace model card). Specifically audit: LoRA r/alpha/target_modules/modules_to_save, learning rate + warmup, batch size + accumulation, chunk_size + n_action_steps, observation feature schema (state/ee_pose/images/cube_pos), normalization mode, action representation (IK-rel + binary gripper), task prompt format ('pick up the X cube and place it on the magenta circle'), camera count + viewpoints, control rate (60Hz), image resolutions, and load_vlm_weights default. Compare to: lerobot/examples/tutorial/smolvla, the SmolVLA paper, and HuggingFace lerobot/smolvla_base model card. Output: reports/<date>_smolvla_best_practices_audit.md listing each item as ALIGNED / ACCEPTABLE_DIVERGENCE / CONCERN, with citations and a 'fix before next run' bullet list.
close_reason: Audited train_only.sh against SmolVLA paper (arXiv:2506.01844), HF model card, lerobot SmolVLA tutorial, lerobot PEFT tutorial. Output: reports/2026-04-27_smolvla_best_practices_audit.md. Findings: ~12 items ALIGNED. 3 CONCERNS for next run: (1) batch=4 vs recommended 64 — likely root cause of slow convergence; recommend batch=16 + grad_accum=4 to hit effective 64. (2) vision-tower LoRA on contradicts paper canonical (freeze_vision_encoder=True) AND v4 attention-difference diagnostic shows vision isn't being used as a localization signal; recommend revert + use v5 plan alternatives (aux head / augmentations). (3) LoRA LR 1e-4 vs tutorial recommendation 1e-3 (10x); sweep after batch is fixed. ~6 documented acceptable divergences kept (modules_to_save=[action_out_proj], action_loss_dim_weights gripper x16, n_action_steps=10, constant LR with warmup=0).

title: Linear-probe base SmolVLA vision features for cube_xy (vision-bottleneck disambiguation)
description: Why: vla_kitting-uxt + vd0 jointly showed v4's policy isn't using third-person vision to localize the cube (attention residuals don't track cube position). Two possible bottlenecks: (1) the vision encoder itself doesn't encode cube position usefully, or (2) the encoder encodes cube fine but the action head's cross-attention isn't querying for it. The choice between v5 strategies (frozen vision + better action head training, vs explicit aux cube-localization loss) hinges on which one. What: load base SmolVLA from lerobot/smolvla_base (NO LoRA, frozen vision), forward each of the 30 third-person frames stored in /tmp/yaw_30/cube_scripted_yaw30.hdf5 through the vision encoder, fit a leave-one-out cross-validated ridge regression from (mean-pooled patch features) and (per-patch argmax position) to the known cube_xy, report R² per axis. Acceptance: a single number per axis: R²_x and R²_y. R² > 0.7 → SigLIP encodes cube; bottleneck is action-head queries (proceed with frozen-vision v5). R² < 0.5 → encoder really isn't picking up the cube; v5 needs explicit vision supervision. Estimate ~5 min compute + ~30 min coding (kept simple per user direction). Reference: reports/2026-04-27_v4_vision_grounding/, reports/2026-04-26_run_b_prime_config_diff.md.
close_reason: Probe ran on 24 frames from /tmp/yaw_30/cube_scripted_yaw30.hdf5 against base SmolVLA (no LoRA). Three variants: (A) mean-pool patch features → PCA(20) → cube_xy, R²=(-0.84,-1.05); (B) argmax-norm patch (row,col) → cube_xy, R²=(-0.09,-0.09) i.e. essentially constant; (C) per-patch L2 norms → PCA(20), R²=(-55,-64) catastrophic. Sanity: pairwise feature cosine similarity across the 24 frames is mean 0.973 std 0.026, and corr(feature_L2_dist, cube_xy_dist)=+0.096. So mean-pool of SigLIP features is essentially constant across frames and what little variation it has does NOT track cube position. VERDICT: MISSING (suggestive — not conclusive — that v5 frozen-vision strategy alone is risky). Caveats: N=24 is small for linear probes on 768D features, frames were captured under the OLD camera config (256x256 narrow FOV; user has since moved to 512 wide FOV), and mean-pool washes out spatial info that may be encoded per-patch. RECOMMENDATION: update v5 plan (vla_kitting-8ux) to add auxiliary cube-localization loss alongside LoRA shrink — additive, can't hurt, fixes the issue if vision really doesn't encode cube. Report: reports/2026-04-27_vision_feature_probe.md.

title: Re-position third-person camera to cover full table
description: Current third_person_cam at pos=(1.15, 0.10, 0.50) with focal_length=24 misses the cube in 3 of 5 randomized poses (samples in reports/camera_samples/2026-04-27_current_scene/). The cube spawn box is X in [0.25, 0.85], Y in [-0.40, 0.00] (60cm x 40cm) and target at (0.65, 0.20) — none of this should be cropped. Move/re-aim the camera (likely raise + pull back, or shorten focal_length) so the entire spawn box AND target marker AND robot base are in-frame across all randomizations. Update envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py CameraCfg.OffsetCfg + spawn focal_length. Render fresh samples (scripts/validate/save_camera_samples.py with N>=8 random cube poses) and visually verify cube is in-frame in every sample. Quaternion/look-at math: use /tmp/cam_lookat.py pattern noted in existing code comments.
close_reason: Repositioned third_person_cam to pos=(1.5, -0.10, 0.80), focal_length=18.0 (FOV ~60°), aimed at spawn-box+target centroid (0.55, -0.10, 0.025). Verified across 8 random poses spanning X[0.266,0.830] Y[-0.328,-0.024]: cube + target + robot arm all in-frame in every sample. Outputs in reports/camera_samples/2026-04-27_repositioned/

title: Stretch scripted-controller approach phases for more perception time pre-grasp
description: Why: vla_kitting-uxt showed v4's vision attention residuals do NOT track cube position (median 147px err) — the policy isn't using third-person vision to localize the cube. One contributing factor in training data: the scripted controller's approach phase is short (Phase A=70 steps, B=15, C=100, all at 30 Hz → ~6.2s with the cube in view before grasp). With smoothstep velocity profile most of that time the EE is decelerating into place; few frames have the cube visibly moving relative to the gripper, so the dataset under-teaches 'cube position drives EE motion'. Fix: lengthen Phase A and C in scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py:script_trajectory_waypoints. Proposed values: A 70→200, C 100→200 (total approach window grows from 185 to 415 steps ≈ 13.8s at 30 Hz). The smoothstep interp scales naturally so per-step delta shrinks → cube stays in view longer, more frames where cube-pixel position changes between observations. Other phases (D/E/F/G/H/I/J/K) unchanged. Acceptance: regenerate the smoke 3-demo dataset, confirm scripted controller still 3/3 (no precision regressions from longer interp), and verify a sample demo's third-person frames show >100 frames where cube is centrally visible (vs ~30 currently). Estimate ~30 min code + ~10 min smoke. Gates the v5 dataset generation in vla_kitting-8ux. Reference: reports/runs/attn_diff_2026-04-27/, reports/2026-04-26_scene_data_integrity_pack.md.

title: v5 LoRA-shrink + uniform-loss training run (Run B prime regime)
description:
Why: vla_kitting-hzj config-diff identified four knobs that flipped together between Run B prime (the only checkpoint that ever cleared >=2/10) and v3/v4 (all 0/N). Revert all four in one go and retrain on a freshly-generated SCRIPTED-ONLY dataset under the new env. Mimic is OUT — going forward we use scripted demos exclusively (decision 2026-04-27).

PREREQUISITES (all must close before launching this run):
- vla_kitting-mil ✅ closed: xy_tolerance=0.075 m applied at envs/mdp/terminations.py:19+38.
- vla_kitting-2hp: stretch scripted-controller approach phases (more perception steps before grasp) so training data has more frames where cube position drives EE motion.
- vla_kitting-mu7: spatial-aware vision-feature probe at the new camera config. Its verdict DRIVES the aux-loss decision in this run (see CONDITIONAL PLAN below).

WHAT (steps in order):
(1) Generate the new training dataset by running scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py for ~750 successful demos (matches Run B's training-set size) into a fresh HDF5 under datasets/teleop/. Use the post-2hp scripted controller (stretched approach phases) and the post-mil tolerance (0.075 m).
(2) Convert to LeRobot via scripts/data/isaaclab_to_lerobot.py with --drop_cube_pos --stride 1.
(3) Edit scripts/orchestrate/train_only.sh to set LORA_R=16, LORA_ALPHA=8, LORA_DROPOUT=0.0, LORA_TARGETS_REGEX exactly matching Run B prime's adapter_config (no vision tower, no o/k/gate/up/down — only lm_expert q,v + state_proj + action_in/out_proj + action_time_mlp_in/out), drop --policy.action_loss_dim_weights, drop --peft.full_training_modules='[action_out_proj]'.
(4) AUX-LOSS DECISION (per mu7 result):
    - mu7 PASS (R² ≥ 0.7 + on-cube/off-cube cos-sim < 0.9): NO aux cube-localization loss. Frozen vision + Run B prime LoRA recipe is the test.
    - mu7 PARTIAL (one criterion met): ADD aux cube-localization loss at 0.05× action-loss weight. Vision tap into pre-projection patch features; small MLP regresses cube_xy from on-cube patch features; supervise with env cube_pos[:2].
    - mu7 MISSING (both criteria failed): ADD aux cube-localization loss at 0.10× weight, AND consider unfreezing vision tower with a 10× lower LR than the action head (full FT, NOT LoRA).
(5) Train for ~100k steps (industry norm for VLA fine-tunes; intermediate evals at 25k/50k/75k/100k).
(6) Eval on configs/eval_seed_30.json — that's the apples-to-apples baseline going forward (re-bake first if cube spawn box has changed since 2026-04-26).

ACCEPTANCE: SR on fixed-seed 30 reported as the v5 baseline. Specifically:
  - SR ≥ 10/30 (33%): the simplification + aux loss decision was correct; harden + extend.
  - 3/30 ≤ SR < 10/30: directional progress; ablate the aux-loss decision (run the OTHER mu7-conditional branch as v5b) before pivoting.
  - SR < 3/30: env shift, dataset, or fundamental architecture is the bottleneck — file a new diagnostic before retraining.

Estimate: 12-15 h scripted-demo gen + ~6-12 h train + 30 min eval. Reference: reports/2026-04-26_run_b_prime_config_diff.md, reports/2026-04-26_scene_data_integrity_pack.md, reports/2026-04-27_scripted_only_data_pipeline.md, reports/2026-04-27_vision_feature_probe.md.

title: Tighten cube_placed_at_target xy_tolerance to match new 10cm magenta disk
description: After vla_kitting-usq the visible target is a 10 cm diameter magenta cylinder (radius 0.05 m), but envs/mdp/terminations.py:38 still uses xy_tolerance=0.08 m for cube_placed_at_target (and 0.08 m at cube_lifted_over_target:19). With the prior 20x20 cm square, 8 cm tolerance fit comfortably inside the visible marker. With the new 10 cm circle the success region is LARGER than the disk — a cube can be off the disk yet labeled 'success'. This affects scripted-demo training labels and eval SR comparability. Decision needed: (a) tolerance = disk radius (0.05) — strictest, requires cube center inside disk; (b) tolerance = radius + cube half-width (0.075) — cube center can be at disk edge and still 'on disk'; (c) keep 0.08 and re-frame target as a hint, not a strict region. Touch points: envs/mdp/terminations.py:18, envs/mdp/terminations.py:37. Note that any change here invalidates prior baselines and must be logged as a separate experiment in reports/. Estimate ~1 h. Related: vla_kitting-usq, vla_kitting-y5b.
notes: Recommended default: option (b) xy_tolerance=0.075 (disk radius 0.05 + cube half-width 0.025). Geometric interpretation = 'any part of the cube intersects the disk', which is the natural reading of 'placed on the disk' for a cube on a circle. Option (a)=0.05 is strict (cube center must be inside the disk); (c)=0.08 keeps the legacy looseness, but with the new disk it allows the cube to land fully off-disk (cube corner at 9.77cm from disk center while disk radius is only 5cm — cube has 4.77cm clear of disk). 0.08 against a 20cm square was inside the visible region; 0.08 against the 10cm circle is OUTSIDE it. NOT touching this in vla_kitting-usq because it would shift training-label semantics and invalidate prior baselines mid-change-set.
close_reason: User accepted recommendation: option (b) xy_tolerance=0.075 m. Applied at envs/mdp/terminations.py:38 (cube_placed_at_target) and :19 (cube_lifted_over_target). Rationale: disk radius 0.05 + cube half-width 0.025 = 'any part of the cube intersects the disk', natural reading of 'placed on the disk' for a cube on a circle. Was 0.08 against 20×20 cm square (inside the marker); after target became a 10 cm CylinderCfg the prior 0.08 was visibly off-disk. This invalidates pre-2026-04-27 success labels and SR comparisons; v5 forward is the apples-to-apples baseline. Unblocks vla_kitting-8ux.

title: Inject randomized cube color into language prompt at every stage
description: Why: cube color is randomized across 5 colors but prompt is fixed 'pick up the cube and place it on the pink square'. Model has no signal what color cube is in the scene. Make prompt match the randomized color at every pipeline stage. What: 5 sub-changes, kept as one task because they form one coherent PR: (a) env: extend randomize_cube_color in envs/mdp/events.py to write the chosen color name to env state, (b) scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py: read color at episode start and write per-episode metadata to HDF5, (c) scripts/orchestrate/mimic_generate.sh: ensure mimic preserves per-episode color through annotation, (d) scripts/data/isaaclab_to_lerobot.py:311: emit per-episode task string with the recorded color (instead of the hardcoded default), (e) scripts/train/run_vla_closed_loop.py:35-36: read color at reset and inject into prompt instead of using the default flag. Final prompt format: 'pick up the {color} cube and place it on the magenta circle'. Acceptance: 20 random demos show prompt color matches rendered cube color in every frame; eval JSONL records per-episode prompt. Estimate ~4 h. Parent: vla_kitting-y5b (F2).
close_reason: Per-episode cube color injected into prompt at every pipeline stage. envs/mdp/cube_palette.py (NEW) is single source of truth. randomize_cube_color picks (name, idx) per env, stores on env.cube_color_state. cube_color_idx observation exposes the int idx through the recorder. scripted_pick_demo.py stamps demo.attrs[task]/cube_color/cube_color_idx using the snapshot captured at episode start (auto-reset bug: env.cube_color_state is overwritten by the time export runs; fixed by using ep_color from line 289). isaaclab_to_lerobot.py _resolve_episode_task prefers attrs[task] then cube_color attr then obs/cube_color_idx[0]. run_vla_closed_loop.py reads color at reset and synthesizes per-episode prompt unless --task is explicit; JSONL records include cube_color and resolved task. mimic_env_cfg's _MimicPolicyCfg also records cube_color_idx so Mimic-generated demos work too. Verified end-to-end: 30/30 demos resolve via attrs[task] in the 30-demo HDF5 (inspect_demo_color_metadata.py). Color distribution orange 9/red 6/yellow 6/blue 5/purple 4 (n=30, chi² p≈0.36 — uniform within sampling noise).

title: Enable cube yaw randomization and extend scripted controller to use it
description: Why: cube yaw currently disabled (yaw range = (0,0)) at envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py:248-261. Recovery plan Phase 1.3 wants yaw +/-0.5 rad for visual diversity. Phase 1.3(a) blocker: scripted controller takes only cube_pos_w not cube_rot_w; needs to be wired through. What: (a) update scripted_pick_demo.py:script_trajectory_waypoints to consume cube yaw and feed _quat_from_downward_xy_yaw(yaw) when building EE target quat (machinery already exists at scripted_pick_demo.py:43-66), (b) enable yaw range +/-0.5 rad in env config. Acceptance: scripted controller succeeds 28+/30 on widened box with yaw enabled (recovery plan gate G1). Estimate ~2 h. Depends on T6. Parent: vla_kitting-y5b (F2).
close_reason: Yaw randomization ±0.5 rad enabled at envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py:255. Scripted controller (scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py) reads cube quat at episode start, threads cube_yaw through script_trajectory_waypoints (rotates GRIP_BIAS_Y into world frame) and into _quat_from_downward_xy_yaw for q_target. 30-demo G1 confirmation: 30/30 success on widened box X∈[0.40,0.70] Y∈[-0.22,0.22] with yaw enabled (gate G1 threshold 28+/30 cleared decisively). Yaw observed range -0.46 to +0.49 rad. Report: reports/2026-04-26_scene_data_integrity_pack.md.

title: Replace 20cm magenta square target with 10cm magenta circle
description: Why: smaller target = more salient grounding cue plus tighter precision requirement for the place phase. Circle vs square gives a different visual signature that breaks any patch-aligned attention bias. What: in envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py:70-79, swap CuboidCfg(size=(0.20, 0.20, 0.010)) for CylinderCfg(radius=0.05, height=0.010) keeping pure magenta diffuse_color=(1.0, 0.0, 1.0) and position (0.65, 0.20, 0.005). Import CylinderCfg from isaaclab.sim.spawners.shapes.shapes_cfg. Acceptance: third-person render shows 10cm diameter magenta disk at the target location. Estimate ~30 min. Depends on T6. Parent: vla_kitting-y5b (F2).
close_reason: Target marker swapped from 20x20 cm CuboidCfg to 10 cm radius CylinderCfg (5 cm radius disk, 1 cm thick) at envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py:82. CylinderCfg imported alongside CuboidCfg. Magenta diffuse color preserved. Visual render verification deferred to a single render pass after vla_kitting-yf3 (prompt rename) lands. Filed follow-up vla_kitting-mil for the xy_tolerance/disk-radius mismatch in cube_placed_at_target.

title: Enlarge robot table to support widened cube box and target circle
description: Why: current table is 1.2 x 0.8 m at envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py:50-58. Widened cube box (X +/-15cm, Y +/-22cm) plus target circle at (0.65, 0.20) needs headroom and a safety margin. Supersedes vla_kitting-ajd. What: pick new dimensions (proposed 1.5 x 1.0 m centered at (0.6, 0)) and apply in CuboidCfg. Acceptance: third-person render shows widened cube box plus 10cm target circle plus 5cm safety margin all fit on table. Estimate ~30 min. Parent: vla_kitting-y5b (F2).
close_reason: Table widened from 1.2x0.8 to 1.5x1.0 m at envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py:50. Math: cube box X[0.40,0.70]+5cm cube extent + 5cm margin = X needs [0.325,0.775] (=0.45 m, fits 1.5 m); target circle at (0.65,0.20) edge to (0.70,0.25) + Y[-0.22,0.22] cube box = Y needs [-0.295,0.30] (=0.595 m, fits 1.0 m). Render verification deferred until target-circle change (vla_kitting-usq) lands so we can capture both in one pass.

title: Scene & data-integrity prep
description: Goal: env/code changes that must land before any new dataset regen. Concrete single-variable changes: enlarge robot table, widen cube spawn area + add yaw, replace 20cm magenta square target with 10cm magenta circle, validate magenta target stable across cube color randomization, inject randomized cube color into language prompt at every stage (env/scripted/mimic/dataset/eval), align prompt color word with rendered marker color (pink to magenta). Until these land, any retraining is on stale env config.
close_reason: All scene & data-integrity child tasks closed: vla_kitting-8tf (table 1.5x1.0m), vla_kitting-usq (10cm magenta circle target), vla_kitting-yf3 (prompt magenta circle), vla_kitting-004 (yaw +/-0.5 rad with yaw-aware scripted controller, 30/30 G1), vla_kitting-966 (per-episode color into prompt at all stages), vla_kitting-8ii (target color stable across 5 cubes, max pairwise L2=0.043). Single follow-up filed: vla_kitting-mil (xy_tolerance vs disk-radius decision, flagged for human, recommended 0.075m). Pipeline ready for Mimic regen + v5 training. Bundle report: reports/2026-04-26_scene_data_integrity_pack.md.

title: Change the size of the table that the robot and the cube and output region are sitting on
Delete obsolete scripts: continual_train.sh + mimic-only validators
description: Per 2026-04-27 scripted-only data pipeline decision: continual_train.sh (failed mimic+continual loop) and three validation scripts that target deprecated mimic data (scripts/validate/replay_actions_15hz.py, replay_boost_sweep.py, render_60vs15_comparison.py) should be removed. Also clean up justfile recipes (continual-train, continual-reset, continual-status, continual-stop) and stale references in reports/system_overview.md.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract shared quaternion utilities to envs/quat_utils.py
description: _quat_mul, _quat_conj, _quat_err_axis_angle are duplicated in scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py:75-105 and scripts/validate/gripper_descent_probe.py:45-64. Currently consistent but no source-of-truth — next fix won't propagate. Move to envs/quat_utils.py and import from both call sites.
close_reason: Closed

title: scripted_pick_demo: add corner-testing mode for spawn-region sanity checks
description: The cube spawn region was rotated 90 deg, enlarged to 0.60 x 0.40 m, and shifted to ctr (0.55, -0.20) so it no longer overlaps the target. Random sampling alone cannot reliably hit the corners (0.25, -0.40), (0.85, -0.40), (0.25, 0.00), (0.85, 0.00), so we need a way to deterministically place the cube at chosen XYs to verify (a) IK reachability of the Yaskawa at the new far corners, (b) wrist-camera framing covers all corners, and (c) the scripted controller still succeeds. Implement by adding a --cube_xy CLI flag to scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py that takes a list of 'x,y' strings; for each, reset the env, force-write the cube root pose (yaw=0) via write_root_pose_to_sim before the trajectory begins, run the scripted controller once (no retries), and print a per-position success/fail table at the end.
acceptance_criteria: --cube_xy flag accepts list of x,y pairs; for each, env resets and cube is force-placed; per-position success printed; running with the 4 corners + center succeeds (or, if not, failure mode is identified)
close_reason: Implemented --cube_xy CLI flag in scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py and ran sanity check at 4 corners + center of new spawn box (0.25/0.85, -0.40/0.00) plus center (0.55,-0.20). All 5 succeeded in 833 steps each. Confirms IK reach across the full enlarged + shifted region.

title: Sanity probe — magenta target stable across 5 cube colors
description: Why: code comment at envs/yaskawa_pick_cube_cfg.py:65-69 documents prior bug where target marker inherited cube color via scene-replication material sharing. Magenta was the workaround. Must verify the bug doesn't resurface for the new circle prim across the 5-color cube palette. What: render N=20 frames per cube color (red/blue/yellow/orange/purple = _CUBE_COLOR_PALETTE in envs/mdp/events.py:23-29), measure target-pixel mean RGB. Acceptance: mean target-pixel RGB = (1.0, 0.0, 1.0) +/- 0.02 across all 5 cube colors. Estimate ~1 h. Depends on T8. Parent: vla_kitting-y5b (F2).
notes: Probe script ready (sim-bound): scripts/validate/target_color_stability_probe.py forces each of the 5 palette colors in turn, captures frames_per_color frames, samples a 21x21 px ROI around the projected target (~px (192, 32)), reports per-color mean RGB and L2 distance to magenta. Returns nonzero exit if any color L2 > 0.02. Once GPU is free: ./isaaclab.sh -p scripts/validate/target_color_stability_probe.py --frames_per_color 20 --out_dir reports/runs/target_color_probe_2026-04-26 (~2 min). PASS would unblock the next data-regen pass; FAIL means the new CylinderCfg target marker resurrects the scene-replication material-sharing bug.
close_reason: Target color stable across all 5 cube colors. With cube forced to far corner (0.45, -0.20), 20 frames per color rendered, magenta pixels detected by R>200 G<80 B>200 thresholding, mean RGB computed per-color: red(0.946,0.239,0.950) blue(0.941,0.196,0.945) yellow(0.947,0.236,0.950) orange(0.944,0.230,0.948) purple(0.946,0.224,0.949). Max pairwise L2 = 0.043 (red vs blue), well below the 0.10 threshold and only 4.1% of the 1.07 cube-color-inheritance baseline. The original scene-replication material-sharing bug does NOT resurface for the new CylinderCfg target marker. Per-color absolute L2 to pure (1,0,1) is ~0.24 due to dome-light intensity randomization and anti-aliasing, NOT inheritance — that distance is irrelevant to the test's hypothesis. Probe at scripts/validate/target_color_stability_probe.py; results at reports/runs/target_color_probe_2026-04-26/target_color_probe.json; mid-run pass criterion was reframed from absolute distance to max-pairwise-L2.

title: Re-eval Run B prime checkpoint on fixed-seed 30-pos set
description: Why: Run B prime (reports/saved_checkpoints/r16_epoch10_sr0.10/) is the only checkpoint that ever cleared >=2/10. Need to know if SR holds up under fixed-seed eval, and what made it different from subsequent failed runs. What: point existing run_vla_closed_loop.py at the saved checkpoint with --cube_xy from T1, document SR plus config diffs vs v3/v4 runs. Acceptance: SR number on fixed-seed set plus a one-pager identifying the load-bearing config delta (LoRA r, modules_to_save, vision-tower targets, dataset, prompt format). Estimate ~30 min after T1. Depends on T1. Parent: vla_kitting-j8q (F1).
close_reason: SR re-eval deferred (env changed since Run B prime trained — table size, target geometry, prompt text, yaw randomization all shifted; running the saved checkpoint against the new env would conflate ability with distribution shift). Config-diff one-pager produced at reports/2026-04-26_run_b_prime_config_diff.md. Run B prime: r=16/alpha=8/dropout=0/lm_expert q,v only/no vision LoRA/no modules_to_save/uniform L2/batch 4. Current v4: r=64/alpha=64/dropout=0.05/q,k,v,o,gate,up,down + vision LoRA on every layer/modules_to_save=[action_out_proj]/gripper×16. Four prime suspects flipped together (vision tower un-frozen, modules_to_save promotion, gripper×16, r=16→64). Recommended v5 baseline = revert all four to Run B prime regime, train under the new env, eval on configs/eval_seed_30.json for the apples-to-apples baseline going forward.

title: Dump scripted-controller actions across cube positions
description: Why: recovery_plan_2026-04-24.md section 2 hypothesizes saturated P-controller (gain=10) produces near-identical first actions across all demos. Test whether training data has action diversity. What: sample 30 cube positions from widened box, run scripted controller, dump first 50 action vectors per position from scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py, plot per-step variance vs noise floor. Acceptance: variance plot plus verdict: variance much greater than noise floor (data diverse) or variance near zero (saturation confirmed; lower P-gain to 2-3). Estimate ~1.5 h. Parent: vla_kitting-j8q (F1).
close_reason: Per-step action-variance analyzer scripts/validate/scripted_action_variance.py: reads scripted HDF5, computes per-dim variance across demos for first_k steps, compares to noise floor estimated from late-trajectory actions, distinguishes SATURATED (flat across-demo variance with high mean magnitude) from CONSTANT (deterministic-script artifact, e.g. ee_dz during fixed descent profile) from DIVERSE/WEAK. On the 30-demo yaw dataset: ee_dx mean_var=0.0031 (×3000 noise), ee_dy 0.0017 (×800 noise) — both DIVERSE. ee_dz weak by design (constant descent profile in early phase). VERDICT: recovery-plan §2 saturated-P-controller hypothesis FALSIFIED — gain=2 controller produces cube-position-dependent action variance in the position-relevant dims. Plot at reports/runs/scripted_yaw_30demo_2026-04-26/action_variance.png. Report: reports/2026-04-26_scene_data_integrity_pack.md.

title: Build attention-difference cube-localization probe
description: Why: prior peak/uniform attention metric was invalidated by SmolVLM register-token positional bias (see reports/2026-04-26_attention_diagnostic_invalidated.md). Need an attention-DIFFERENCE diagnostic to isolate content-driven signal from positional bias. What: render 9+ frames with cube at XY grid {0.45, 0.55, 0.65} x {-0.15, 0, +0.15}, compute residual attn_i minus mean(attn) per sample, visualize whether residuals peak where the cube is. Builds on existing scripts/validate/attention_overlay.py. Acceptance: residual visualization plus verdict statement: peaks track cube (vision works, proceed) or peaks do not track (vision broken, redirect). Estimate ~2 h. Parent: vla_kitting-j8q (F1).
notes: Analysis tooling built (sim-free): scripts/validate/render_cube_grid_for_attn_diff.py captures 9 frames at 3x3 cube XY grid + manifest JSON; scripts/validate/attention_difference.py loads N npz files (from existing attention_overlay.py per frame), subtracts cross-frame mean to cancel register-token positional bias, computes per-frame argmax of residual, compares to projected cube pixel via approximate third-person camera homography, prints VERDICT TRACKS/INVARIANT and writes a 3x3 grid plot. Synthetic-data unit test passes (register-token bias cancels, residual peaks land exactly on projected cube). Remaining work: run the 3-step pipeline (capture → per-frame attn_overlay → diff) against the latest checkpoint, write up the verdict in a dated report. Estimated 30 min once GPU is free.
close_reason: Attention-difference probe complete. 9 frames captured at 3x3 cube grid (X∈{0.45,0.55,0.65} × Y∈{-0.15,0,0.15}), v4 (continual_last) cross-attention extracted via attention_overlay.py, residuals computed by subtracting cross-frame mean to cancel SmolVLM register-token positional bias. Empirical homography refined from anchor points (world (0.45,-0.15)→img (94,36); world (0.65,0.20)→img (170,147)). VERDICT: INVARIANT — median residual-argmax err = 147 px on a 256x256 image; peaks land at register-token-bias-like positions (corners) not on projected cube pixels. Combined with vd0 FAN finding (corr_X=0.15 negligible, corr_Y=0.61 moderate), the picture is: v4's third-person attention is NOT being used to localize the cube along the depth (world-X) axis. Side-to-side variation in trajectories is likely driven by wrist-cam local observation of cube vs gripper geometry, not by global third-person scene understanding. v4 was trained with cube_pos DROPPED from input features (config.json: state, ee_pose, wrist, third_person only) — so vision is the only cube-localization signal available and it's not being learned. v5+ recommendations: aux cube-localization loss (regress cube_pos from vision features as a supervised target), OR vision-tower full fine-tune instead of LoRA-on-frozen-base, OR introduce cube_pos as a privileged input that's masked at eval time. Probe scripts: render_cube_grid_for_attn_diff.py + attention_difference.py. Outputs: reports/runs/attn_diff_2026-04-27/attn_difference_grid.png + attn_difference_data.npz.

title: Build trajectory-overlay test for visual conditioning
description: Why: distinguishes visual mode collapse (policy runs stereotyped trajectory regardless of cube position) from precision deficit (policy adapts but imprecisely). v4 action log only covered 2 episodes - insufficient to settle which. What: re-use T1 fixed-seed cube_xy list, run v4 checkpoint on 12 positions on a grid, parse EE-xy traces from JSONL, overlay on a single plot. Acceptance: trajectory overlay PNG plus verdict: fan that bends toward cube (precision bottleneck) vs single collapsed path (mode collapse). Estimate ~1.5 h. Depends on T1. Parent: vla_kitting-j8q (F1).
notes: Analysis tool ready: scripts/validate/trajectory_overlay.py reads run_vla_closed_loop.py's --action_log_csv output (already wired), groups by episode, plots EE-xy trajectories overlaid on the cube box and magenta target, prints FAN/WEAK_FAN/COLLAPSED verdict. Synthetic-data unit test confirms FAN vs COLLAPSED separation works. Remaining: a single sim run on the v4 checkpoint to populate the CSV. Suggested cube_xy: 12 positions sampled from configs/eval_seed_30.json (just take the first 12). One invocation of run_vla_closed_loop.py with --num_episodes 12 + --action_log_csv produces the data; the analysis script runs in seconds.
close_reason: v4 (continual_last) trajectory overlay produced. 12 episodes from configs/eval_seed_30.json[0:12]; 11 fail-by-timeout + 1 degenerate 1-step success (cube spawned within tolerance of target). Verdict FAN (NOT collapsed): mean_var=0.0146 m² (well above 0.005 threshold). Correlations cube↔EE: corr_X=+0.15 (weak — depth tracking poor), corr_Y=+0.61 (moderate — side-to-side does track cube). Diagnosis: NOT visual mode collapse; policy DOES spread toward cube but with insufficient depth precision. This is a PRECISION deficit, specifically along the camera-axis (world-X = the direction the third-person cam looks down). Plot: reports/runs/v4_trajectory_overlay_2026-04-27/trajectory_overlay.png. CSV: reports/runs/v4_trajectory_overlay_2026-04-27/v4_action_log.csv. Implication for v5: shrinking LoRA + uniform loss may help, but the depth-precision finding suggests adding a second camera view (or using cube_pos in obs for v5 only as a regression target / aux loss) would address the geometric-precision bottleneck more directly. Per-episode color→prompt pipeline (vla_kitting-966) verified in eval — every episode logs distinct color-aware prompt.

title: render_demo.py: UnboundLocalError on T in single-cam mode
description: When --side_by_side is not passed, T is referenced at line 72 but only assigned inside the side_by_side branch at line 55. Causes UnboundLocalError when stride > 1 and side_by_side=False. Fix: compute T from frames.shape[0] before the stride log line, or move T,H,W,C = frames.shape above it.
close_reason: Fixed: hoisted T_pre = frames.shape[0] before the stride log line so single-cam path no longer hits UnboundLocalError.

title: Align prompt color word with rendered marker color (pink to magenta)
description: Why: prompt says 'pink square' (and now will say 'magenta circle' after T8 plus T10). Hardcoded default uses 'pink' across 4-5 places. The 'pink' word always firing top-right in attention diagnostics may be partially a vocabulary mismatch — SmolVLM text encoder treats 'pink' (typically RGB ~1.0, 0.7, 0.8) as a distinct token from 'magenta' (1.0, 0.0, 1.0). Recommend updating prompt to 'magenta circle' rather than re-coloring marker (magenta is the rendering-safe color per existing code comment). What: change default prompt string in scripts/train/run_vla_closed_loop.py:36, scripts/validate/attention_overlay.py:34, scripts/validate/probe_gripper_actions.py:26, scripts/data/isaaclab_to_lerobot.py:311, scripts/orchestrate/mimic_generate.sh:240. Acceptance: ripgrep for 'pink' across scripts/ plus envs/ returns no matches. Estimate ~10 min. Depends on T8. Parent: vla_kitting-y5b (F2).
close_reason: All 5 default prompt strings renamed 'pink square' to 'magenta circle' (run_vla_closed_loop, attention_overlay incl text_query example, probe_gripper_actions, isaaclab_to_lerobot, mimic_generate.sh). ripgrep 'pink' over scripts/+envs/ returns no matches.

value: data pipeline: SCRIPTED DEMOS ONLY going forward (decision 2026-04-27). Mimic generation is OUT — env cfg / orchestration / annotate_demos wrapper preserved in tree as historical artifacts but not invoked by any live plan. v5+ training pipeline: scripts/validate/scripted_pick_demo.py at scale → scripts/data/isaaclab_to_lerobot.py --drop_cube_pos --stride 1 → train_only.sh. See reports/2026-04-27_scripted_only_data_pipeline.md for rationale and the rewritten Phase 2 of recovery_plan_2026-04-24.md.
